commands.review_summary:
The PR filters `sessions.files.list` so workspace-external touched files are omitted from the workspace rail list while genuinely missing workspace-internal files remain marked missing.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main maps outside-root touched paths to `missing: true` and the UI renders that flag as the missing badge. I did not run a live Control UI or macOS reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer confirmation of narrowed Gateway list semantics, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(sessions-files): exclude workspace-external files from workspace rail list
target.body:
Fixes #95685

## What Problem This Solves

The Session Workspace Rail (right sidebar, added in 2026.6.9) marks files outside the configured workspace directory as "missing" (red badge), even though those files exist and were successfully read during the session. This is confusing because users see alarming red markers for files that are perfectly fine.

## Evidence

### Fix

In `buildListResult()`, filter out files that are outside the configured workspace directory before returning the file list. The check uses the existing `resolveTouchedFilePath()` to determine whether a file path resolves inside the workspace root.

- Files inside the workspace that genuinely don't exist on disk still show as `missing: true` ✓
- Files outside the workspace (e.g. `/usr/lib/node_modules/...`) are excluded from the list ✓
- The browser explorer view is unaffected — it only shows workspace-internal files anyway

### Tests

All 30 existing tests pass (no behavior change for workspace-internal files):

```
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  30 passed (30)
```

### Checks
- Format: `oxfmt` ✓
- Lint: `oxlint` ✓
- Diff: +19 source, -3 source (22 lines)
- Branch: clean from `origin/main`
